published_prior_art.cite: VeitchSridharBlei2020 and ImaiNakamura2024TextTreatments: exact causally sufficient representations support point identification/estimation

repo_prior_art.cite: doc/API.md §3 is only a seed PartialID catalogue; pid_network_exposure_v1 concerns shared graph completions, not learned-representation distortion

published_prior_art.cite: BeresteanuMolchanovMolinari2012 random-set partial identification; Ura2018 mismeasured-treatment bounds; HorowitzManski1995 corrupted-data bounds
FongGrimmer2023 shows randomized texts alone do not identify latent-treatment effects; EgamiFongGrimmerRobertsStewart2022 warns about representation-dependent text causal designs
BeresteanuMolchanovMolinari2012 and Manski2003: generic interval/random-set degeneracy logic, but not epsilon-fiber terminology

structure_flags.label: Introduction
structure_flags.one_line: §4 has five narrative paragraphs, not the required six explicit sub-paragraphs; the 'how research benefits from the conclusion' item is merged into implications.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship; the survey needs a sharper separation from generic corrupted-data/bounded-measurement-error bounds such as HorowitzManski1995, not only text-embedding papers.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: VeitchSridharBlei2020 is an observational-text adjustment workflow, not a text-as-treatment endpoint workflow; the consumer/target should be tied more directly to Imai-Nakamura, Fong-Grimmer, or Corcoll-Andreu et al.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The assumptions do not force any admissible calibration c: supp(Z)->Z to hit r1 or r0; if a target oracle value has no observed preimage, m(r_j) is unconstrained rather than bounded by extrema of mu over F_epsilon(r_j).

soundness_flags.label: Proof-sketch route
soundness_flags.one_line: The outer-containment step asserts that an oracle target r_j must map to an observed Z in F_epsilon(r_j), but that inverse-reachability condition is not stated in §7.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add an explicit target-reachability or inverse-calibration assumption, or redefine the estimand as a fiber-selected oracle response rather than m(r) at arbitrary oracle coordinates.
Separate the flagship claim from generic bounded-measurement-error/random-set bounds by proving a high-dimensional representation-specific generic obstruction, not just the ballwise interval formula.

Replace the Veitch-as-text-treatment framing with a quoted target claim from Imai-Nakamura, Fong-Grimmer, or Corcoll-Andreu et al. that actually uses structured treatments or latent treatment representations.

recommended_next_step: Revise the estimand/calibration assumptions first, then sharpen the published-axis comparison enough to justify a flagship-tier target.
